name: Bulgur Stuffed Cabbage
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the core from the cabbage and steam it until all leaves are soft and separate easily.
2. In a pan, sauté parsley, onions, celery, Italian seasoning, and garlic in an oil substitute until the onions are soft.
3. Add 2 cans of tomato sauce, 4 cups of water, and bulgur to the pan. Cook for about 1/2 hour over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the bulgur is tender.
4. Remove from heat and prepare cabbage leaves for stuffing:
a. Place a spoonful of mixture in the center of each leaf.
b. Starting at one side, roll the leaf up and fold the ends under.
5. Arrange the stuffed leaves in a deep baking pan.
6. Mix 1 can of tomato sauce with 1/2 cup water to create a liquid for moistening the cabbage during baking.
7. Pour the mixture over the stuffed cabbage leaves to keep them moist.
8. Bake at 375°F for about 30 minutes until the cabbage is hot.
